research.interface: Blob Storage REST API with x-ms-version 2026-04-06: Put Block, Put Block List, Get Block List, conditional headers

official_sources.title: Put Block (REST API) - Azure Storage
official_sources.used_for: PUT ?comp=block&blockid= URI shape, block id rules (valid Base64, <= 64 bytes before encoding, URL-encoded, same length for every block in a blob), required Authorization/Date-or-x-ms-date/x-ms-version/Content-Length headers, 201 success, block sizes/limits, uncommitted-block garbage collection, and the fact that Put Block is absent from the conditional-headers support table

official_sources.title: Put Block List (REST API) - Azure Storage
official_sources.used_for: PUT ?comp=blocklist, the BlockList XML body with Committed/Uncommitted/Latest lookup elements (Latest searches uncommitted first, then committed), element order defining blob content, x-ms-blob-content-type, conditional-header support, 201 with quoted ETag, and cleanup of unnamed uncommitted blocks

official_sources.title: Get Block List (REST API) - Azure Storage
official_sources.used_for: GET ?comp=blocklist&blocklisttype=committed|uncommitted|all (committed is the default), the BlockList/CommittedBlocks/UncommittedBlocks/Block/Name/Size response XML, alphabetical uncommitted ordering with most-recent-content-per-id, and ETag returned only when committed blocks exist

official_sources.title: Versioning for Azure Storage
official_sources.used_for: 2026-04-06 as the latest fully deployed service version (2026-06-06 not yet enabled in every region), the YYYY-MM-DD x-ms-version format, and the rule that OAuth-authorized requests must pass x-ms-version 2017-11-09 or later

official_sources.title: Specifying conditional headers for Blob service operations
official_sources.used_for: If-Match/If-None-Match semantics, 412 Precondition Failed for every unmet write condition, If-None-Match: * performing the operation only when the resource does not exist, and the operations table showing Put Block List supports conditional headers while Put Block does not

research.language_rationale: TypeScript on Node's built-in fetch: the block-blob staging protocol is defined at the REST level (query parameters, XML bodies, x-ms-version), and the uploader's whole job -- stable block ids, resume from the uncommitted list, ETag preconditions -- is exactly the layer @azure/storage-blob would abstract away. Raw REST with zero dependencies is idiomatic for a Node tool and keeps the researched contract observable in tests.

verified_facts: Put Block: PUT {blob-url}?comp=block&blockid={id}; the block id is a Base64 string, at most 64 bytes before encoding, URL-encoded in the query, and every block of a blob must use the same pre-encoded id length.
Put Block succeeds with 201 Created; Authorization, Date or x-ms-date, x-ms-version, and Content-Length are required; Put Block supports no conditional headers.

Put Block List: PUT {blob-url}?comp=blocklist with an XML BlockList body; Latest resolves an id from the uncommitted list first and the committed list second; the XML element order defines the committed blob's content; success is 201 with a quoted ETag.

Put Block List supports If-Match/If-None-Match/If-Modified-Since/If-Unmodified-Since; any unmet write condition answers 412 Precondition Failed (storage error code ConditionNotMet), and If-None-Match: * commits only when the blob does not exist yet.
x-ms-blob-content-type on Put Block List stores the blob's content type; omitted properties are cleared on successful commits.

Get Block List: GET {blob-url}?comp=blocklist&blocklisttype=committed|uncommitted|all, defaulting to committed; the response nests Block elements with Name and Size under CommittedBlocks/UncommittedBlocks; uncommitted blocks list alphabetically with only the most recent upload per id; ETag is returned only when the blob has committed blocks.

Uncommitted blocks are garbage-collected a week after the last successful Put Block when never committed, and immediately dropped from storage when a commit omits them; re-staging an existing uncommitted id replaces its content at the next commit.
The latest fully deployed storage service version is 2026-04-06 (2026-06-06 exists but is not enabled in all regions); OAuth-authorized requests must carry x-ms-version 2017-11-09 or newer.
Storage errors use an XML body <Error><Code/><Message/></Error>; committing a block id the service cannot find fails with 400 InvalidBlockList.
